prompt_quality.reasoning: Iter 3 adds a substantive 35-line behavioral-anchor scoring rubric (agent.py:216-250) for all 8 dimensions, with explicit anti-default-to-3/4 guidance and fallback instruction for insufficient evidence — this is real prompt-engineering quality, not filler. The Voice Check (agent.py:252-257) from iter 2 is retained. Reasoning-before-commit ordering is now stated in prompt text ('Each score is `{reason, score}`' at agent.py:302), though the enforced responseSchema still has {score, reason} — see llm_configuration. However, the long-standing redundancy issues persist: competitor rule is restated 4× (SYSTEM_PROMPT lines 46-52 + Known Competitors header agent.py:195 + plain-text fallback builder gemini.py:472 + enrich_persona gemini.py:521-526), EVALUATE-not-EXPAND still 3× (SYSTEM_PROMPT lines 23-37 + execution instructions items 7-10 at agent.py:270-273 + reflection-loop bullets at agent.py:292-294), and SYSTEM_PROMPT is still duplicated between gemini.py:394-405 systemInstruction and agent.py:169-170 packet markdown (prefix-caching-unfriendly). Structure is strong (role + framework + constraints + rubric + reflection + output) but high-attention text is diluted by repetition.

llm_configuration.reasoning: Iter 3 delivers the highest-leverage fix from the iter 1+2 backlog: the ~80-line embedded schema JSON block is DELETED from build_review_packet (agent.py:301-302, replaced by a single prose summary). This resolves the Google-documented anti-pattern of schema-in-prompt-and-responseSchema-simultaneously. Temperature (0.1) + topP (0.95) set in GeminiConfig (gemini.py:45-46) and threaded to all builders via _base_generation_config (gemini.py:375-381) — correct. responseMimeType='application/json' on all 3 builders; responseSchema attached on builders 2 and 3 (gemini.py:429-430, 461-462). Builder 1 legitimately omits responseSchema because google_search is incompatible with responseSchema on Gemini (noted at gemini.py:390-391) — correct architectural call. NEW ISSUE: prompt now says '`{reason, score}`' but the responseSchema built from review-output-schema.json still orders {score, reason} (lines 23-31) — _schema_from_template at agent.py:115,117 sets `required` and `propertyOrdering` from list(value.keys()) so the schema WILL emit score-before-reason at decode time. Prompt-text and responseSchema now contradict each other on score ordering; the constrained-decoder wins, defeating CoT. Also: both stages hardcoded gemini-2.5-pro (service.py:17, 86, 140) — flash tier would save ~5-10x on persona enrichment. Three divergent builder shapes still retained.

retry_reliability.reasoning: No changes in iter 3 — retry infrastructure remains excellent. Three-class error hierarchy (gemini.py:14-31), full-jitter exponential backoff capped at 30s (gemini.py:49-51), _MAX_ATTEMPTS=3 (gemini.py:34), Retry-After honored on 429 (gemini.py:54-60, 330), SAFETY/RECITATION/BLOCKLIST/PROHIBITED_CONTENT treated as permanent (gemini.py:263-266). Semantic validators (_check_evidence_grounding + _check_competitor_leak, gemini.py:116-203) run post-parse with retry-note injection (gemini.py:299-302) and last-attempt bypass (gemini.py:296-297) — a rare and correct design pattern. enrich_persona uses the same error classification (gemini.py:594-605). Residual polish items from prior iters: builder-index-coupled-to-attempt-index rather than builder-rotation-on-semantic-failure; Retry-After HTTP-date form (RFC-7231) not parsed (only numeric seconds at gemini.py:57-58) — neither is critical.

pipeline_structure.reasoning: Unchanged in iter 3. The two headline structural issues persist: (1) duplicate fetch_webpage_context — once at service.py:18 for the review, again at service.py:90 for persona enrichment, doubling crawl latency and network cost per end-to-end request with no lru_cache or threading between stages; (2) no persona cache keyed by (service, type, description) hash, so identical inputs re-spend full LLM calls. Schema placement improved by iter 3 (removed from prompt), which is a structural win, but it is captured under llm_configuration. Post-parse semantic validators (iter 2) remain well-threaded through service.py:45-52 with webpage_context + allowed_competitors forwarded from the same crawl. Net: caching layer and call-topology cleanup remain the dominant unaddressed structural gaps.

token_efficiency.reasoning: First activation of this criterion in the late phase — the iter 3 diff is largely a token-efficiency play. The ~80-line JSON schema dump (json.dumps(schema, indent=2) formerly at the end of build_review_packet) is removed, saving roughly 500-700 tokens per call; the 35-line scoring rubric added in its place costs roughly 200-300 tokens. Net saving per call: ~300-500 tokens, and the rubric is genuine quality content, not pure bloat. However, the large remaining wasters are untouched: (a) SYSTEM_PROMPT is sent TWICE (systemInstruction at gemini.py:394-405 + packet markdown at agent.py:169-170) — ~300-500 duplicated tokens/call and it breaks prompt-cache-prefix stability; (b) competitor rule appears in 4 locations — ~200-300 tokens of repetition/call; (c) webpage excerpt still hardcoded at MAX_PAGES=8 × MAX_EXCERPT_CHARS=2200 ≈ 4.4k tokens (webpage.py:14-15) with no relevance filter / top-K selection / summarization; (d) EVALUATE-not-EXPAND restated 3× ≈ 150-300 tokens. Score lifts from 2.5 baseline to 3.25 because the single largest easily-removable block (the embedded schema) is finally gone.

feedback: Iter 3 delivered the single highest-leverage fix from the three-iteration backlog — the embedded 80-line JSON schema dump in build_review_packet is DELETED and replaced with a one-line prose summary of top-level keys. This resolves the Google-documented schema-in-prompt-and-responseSchema anti-pattern on the prompt side and reclaims an estimated 300-500 net tokens per call (after offsetting the new 35-line scoring rubric). The scoring rubric itself is substantive: behavioral anchors at 1/3/5 for all 8 dimensions with explicit anti-default-to-3 guidance. Net: +0.25 prompt_quality, +0.25 llm_configuration, +0.75 token_efficiency (on first activation at late phase). A new bug was introduced that needs attention: the prompt now declares each score is '`{reason, score}`' (agent.py:302) but review-output-schema.json still defines scores as {score, reason} (lines 24-31), and _schema_from_template (agent.py:109-117) propagates this ordering into the responseSchema's propertyOrdering and required arrays. The constrained decoder wins at decode time — so CoT reasoning-before-commit is asserted in the prompt but violated by the enforced schema. A one-line fix in review-output-schema.json (swap field order) makes the contradiction disappear. Remaining backlog from prior iters all carries forward: duplicate fetch_webpage_context, no persona cache, hardcoded gemini-2.5-pro for both stages, SYSTEM_PROMPT duplication across systemInstruction + packet markdown, competitor rule 4×, EVALUATE-not-EXPAND 3×, three divergent builder shapes.

priority_fixes.suggestion: Reorder each score object in review-output-schema.json from {"score": 1, "reason": "string"} to {"reason": "string", "score": 1} (lines 24-31, all 8 dimensions). _schema_from_template at agent.py:115,117 propagates dict order into propertyOrdering AND required — fixing the source file aligns the enforced responseSchema with the reasoning-before-commit order the prompt now declares at agent.py:302. Without this fix the prompt and enforced schema contradict each other on field order, and the constrained decoder overrides the prompt, defeating the CoT win.

Eliminate the duplicate crawl per end-to-end request. Simplest viable patch: wrap fetch_webpage_context with @functools.lru_cache(maxsize=64) in webpage.py (the function is purely URL-keyed and already side-effect-free). Alternative: thread webpage_context through service.generate_persona_from_form → service.create_brief_from_form → service.build_packet_for_brief so the form handler fetches once and both persona enrichment and the review consume the same snapshot. Either cuts one full HTTP crawl (often several seconds and ~4.4k tokens of duplicate work) per end-to-end review.

Stop duplicating SYSTEM_PROMPT. Currently it sits in gemini.py:394-405 as systemInstruction (builder 1 only) AND is embedded in the packet markdown at agent.py:169-170 (consumed by all 3 builders). Fix: make SYSTEM_PROMPT exclusively live in systemInstruction on all 3 builders (add systemInstruction to build_request_inline_prompt and build_request_plain_text_fallback), and remove the '## System Prompt' block from build_review_packet. Saves ~300-500 tokens/call and makes the prompt prefix stable for provider prompt-caching (Gemini/Anthropic-style cached-input 90% discount).

Consolidate duplicated rules to one canonical location each. (a) Competitor rule: keep only in SYSTEM_PROMPT (agent.py:46-52); delete the 'Known Competitors (the ONLY product names you may mention)' header text at agent.py:195 (replace with plain 'Known Competitors'), delete the extra competitor sentence at gemini.py:472 in plain_text_fallback, and delete the 'CRITICAL RULE ON COMPETITORS' block in enrich_persona at gemini.py:521-526 (replace with one line referencing the rule). (b) EVALUATE-not-EXPAND: keep only in SYSTEM_PROMPT (agent.py:23-37); delete execution-instruction items 7-10 (agent.py:270-273) and reflection-loop bullets that restate it (agent.py:292-294). Repeated rules do not strengthen instruction-following and dilute attention on the rubric and severity guidance that actually drive quality.

Downgrade persona enrichment to a flash-tier model. Currently service.py:17, 86, and 140 all default to 'gemini-2.5-pro'. Persona enrichment is a short structured-output task that does not need pro-tier reasoning. Split the default: keep pro for run_review (critical reasoning over the full packet) but use 'gemini-2.5-flash' (or 'gemini-2.5-flash-latest') for enrich_persona. Expected ~5-10x cost reduction on the persona stage per request; no observed quality loss for persona-cards at flash tier.

Compress the webpage excerpt. MAX_PAGES=8 × MAX_EXCERPT_CHARS=2200 ≈ 17.6k chars (~4.4k tokens) per call with no relevance filter. Add a top-K relevance filter (simple: TF-IDF / BM25 over the persona's goals+pain_points+core_journey) and cap each page to the top 1-2 paragraphs that match. Even a naive heuristic — keep only paragraphs containing hero/CTA/pricing/benefit/signup/login terms — typically cuts 50-70% of the excerpt with no quality loss, since most pages have large footers and boilerplate that add no evaluative signal.

Two small polish items. (a) Decouple builder selection from the API-attempt index. Currently REQUEST_BUILDERS[min(attempt, len-1)] means a semantic-validation failure on attempt 0 still uses builder 0 on attempt 1, never rotating to the responseSchema-backed inline builder. Rotate on ANY retry reason (API error OR semantic failure) — on validation failure, advancing to builder 2 (inline, has responseSchema) has the best chance of fixing a structural issue. (b) _parse_retry_after at gemini.py:54-60 only handles numeric seconds. RFC-7231 allows HTTP-date form — add email.utils.parsedate_to_datetime as a fallback for completeness.
